message:
fix(tui): use list_state scroll_offset for mouse click targeting

The message browser and search cockpit screens were computing the
visible row range from viewport_range(results.len(), list_height, cursor)
for mouse click handling. This helper computes a theoretical viewport
window, but the actual rendered scroll position is tracked by the
ListState widget's internal scroll_offset(). When the user scrolled via
mouse wheel or other means that updated ListState but not the cursor,
the two values diverged, causing mouse clicks to select the wrong item
or be silently ignored.

Both screens (messages.rs, search.rs) now read the authoritative
scroll offset from self.list_state.borrow().scroll_offset() and use
self.results.len() as the upper bound instead of the viewport_range end,
ensuring mouse clicks always map to the correct visible row.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
